title: Inula limonella
intro: Inula limonella, also known as Lemon helichrysum, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Inula limonella include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Inula limonella is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into creams and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Inula limonella is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Inula, which includes various species of plants commonly known as elecampane or furze. The genus Inula is further classified under the subfamily Asteroideae. Inula limonella is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant's classification is consistent with its botanical characteristics and distribution.
